mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 8.0 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates sharply and recession probabilities rise, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0, or the signal loses predictive power during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts signal synchronized consumer and construction demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or positive returns occur in less than 50 percent of co-movement instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during late-cycle phases, or 5-day forward returns show negative correlation with INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent annually confirms sustained economic expansion, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 60 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above index level 230 forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite CPIAUCSL exceeding 230, or the 5-day return correlation with inflation turns positive.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The payroll-unemployment divergence fails to predict returns, or forward returns are negative in greater than 55 percent of divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal supply-side contraction and weakening business investment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Markets ignore supply contractions and post positive 60-day returns in greater than 65 percent of decline episodes.
